msgs.text:
Turn 34 — the d=8 A2 -secondary bottleneck is killed by the θ80 -quotient I used only the current attachments. The standing problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the two one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH, with canonical maps to G, as stated in the attached prompt and problem statement.

The new conclusion is:B8↠H8(2).

So the next A2=R/(4,t) secondary coefficient bottleneck identified in Turn 33 also dies.Recall the setup from Turns 30–33. In the local Ef-coefficient ring

R≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,the dangerous row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).Thus R/(4,t) is the first 2-Witt/Bockstein coefficient direction, while R/(2,t2) is the first t-derivation direction. Turn 32 cleared the first t-layer, and Turn 30 reduced the first A2 -secondary obstruction toHd(2):=Ld[2]/imθd0,withθd0:ker(Ld⊕Ld(u,v)↦[A,u]+[B,v]Ld+1)→Ld[2],θd0 (u,v)=u[2]+v[2].

Turn 30 recorded dimH8(2)=21, and Turn 33 left exactly this d=8 quotient as the next real first-A2 test.The key point is that d=8 looks exceptional only before passing to the secondary quotient H8(2).LetV:=L8[2].From the residual degree-16 calculation, the high torsor image in V is the codimension-one hyperplane

M=kerλ,where λ=coeff ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coeff(B8)[2].Equivalently, Turn 11 found rank 35 inside the 36-dimensional square quotient L8[2], with this λ as the missing functional.Now observe that the missing λ-direction is already killed by the A2 -secondary quotient.

Indeed, take u=0,v=B8∈L8.

Since we are in characteristic 2,[B,B8]=BB8+B8B=0.

Therefore(0,B8)∈ker(L8⊕L8[A,−]+[B,−]L9),and henceθ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].Butλ((B8)[2])=1.Soimθ80⊆kerλ=M.

Since M is a hyperplane in V, this immediately gives

M+imθ80=V.

Passing to the quotientH8(2)=V/imθ80,the image of M is therefore all of H8(2):M=H8(2).

Thus the combined coefficient map at d=8 is surjective:B8↠H8(2).This is exactly the structural test Turn 33 asked for: do not compute a fixed H8 -tail; ask whether the high image, after quotienting by the zero-2-ghost secondary directions, covers H8(2). It does. Turn 33 had already shown B5,B6,B7 are onto their secondary quotients by domination from the residual square-surjectivity maps at degrees 10,12,14.

So the first A2=R/(4,t) secondary tower is now clear through d=8:H4(2)dies by Turn 31,H5(2),H6(2),H7(2)die by Turn 33,H8(2)dies by the quotient argument above.The conceptual reason is worth isolating. Residually, degree 16 was exceptional because the high torsor missed exactly one λ-direction. But coefficient-theoretically, the A2 -secondary quotient has already modded out by the square-neutral zero-2-ghost direction represented by the section-cocycle correction q↦qY8.

That direction contributes (B8)[2]to imθ80, and (B8)[2]is not in the residual high hyperplane. Hence the residual codimension-one miss disappears after passing to H8(2). This is the same section-cocycle mechanism first seen at degree 4, where q↦qY4 had zero 2-ghost and supplied the old missing B8 -shadow.This does not yet prove the full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma over all Artinian quotients of R≃Z2 [[t]]. It clears the entire first A2 -secondary tower through the first exceptional root d=8, but higher coefficient layers such as 4, 2t, and t2 still require an iterated ghost/Bockstein argument. Turn 32 explicitly warned that higher m=(2,t)-adic layers can reintroduce square interactions even though the first t-layer is contractible.

ESTABLISHED: The d=8 first A2=R/(4,t) secondary coefficient obstruction dies. If M=kerλ⊂L8[2]is the residual high-torsor image, then imθ80⊂M, because θ80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]and λ((B8)[2])=1. Hence M+imθ80=L8[2], so B8↠H8(2).

GAP: This still does not prove finite coefficient nonemptiness over every Artinian quotient of R. The first t-layer and the first A2 -secondary layers through d=8 are clear, but the iterated coefficient layers 4,2t,t2,… remain to be organized into a uniform m=(2,t)-adic ghost induction.

NEXT: Prove the general first-A2 statement Bd↠Hd(2)for all d≥9 using the residual induction Cd=Ld, then lift the argument from the first coefficient layer to the next m-adic layer, starting with the coupled 4, 2t, and t2 ghosts.[TURN34-H8-A2-SECONDARY-CLEARED]
